revision.text:
[[de:Flachsernte (Altes Ägypten)]] ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Flax Harvest |NAME2 = G43-V28-G1-A25-D40-M37
Weha-mehau
''Wḥ3-mḥˁw''
''Uprooting the flax plants'' /
''Striking the flax plants'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Ancient Egypt)|ja|古王国 (古代エジプト)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME = G47-X1-A24-M37
Tjat-mehau
''ṯ3t-mḥˁw''
''Sorting of {{ill|Flax|ja|亜麻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flax|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマ (植物)|1=Flax|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q45108}} plants'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = |NAME3 = G47-X1-A24-M38
Tjat-mehau
''ṯ3t-mḥˁw''
''Pulling out the flax plants'' |NAME3-ERWEITERT = |BILD1 = Gemeiner_flachs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Common flax }} The '''{{ill|Flax harvest (Ancient Egypt)|ja|古代エジプトの亜麻の収穫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flax harvest (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' was a central aspect of {{ill|Agriculture in ancient Egypt|ja|古代エジプトの農業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agriculture in ancient Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Funerary cult|ja|葬祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funerary cult|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠婚葬祭|1=The Four Ceremonial Occasions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855444}}. The best-documented older depictions were found in the {{ill|World Chamber of the Sun Temple of Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー太陽神殿#世界の間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Chamber of the Sun Temple of Niuserre|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2455 to 2420 BC). In the {{ill|New Kingdom (Ancient Egypt)|ja|新王国 (古代エジプト)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the flax harvest was a popular {{ill|Iconography|en|Iconography|qid=Q208145|lt=iconographic|1=Iconography}} motif in connection with {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|qid=Q618140|lt=Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru}} in the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|死者の書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|1=Livre des morts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q441463}}. ==Flax Cultivation and Harvest== The foundation of agricultural planning was the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの太陰暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had the advantage over the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian administrative calendar|ja|古代エジプトの行政暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian administrative calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Season|en|Season|qid=Q24384|lt=seasons|1=Season}} did not shift through the calendar year. ===Cultivation=== Since flax cultivation did not require specific soil conditions, it could be grown beyond the banks of the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|qid=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}} in ancient Egypt. Another prerequisite was a good water supply during the main growth phase in {{ill|February|en|February|qid=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}/{{ill|March|en|March|qid=Q110|lt=March|1=March}}. The {{ill|Sowing|en|Sowing|qid=Q777671|lt=sowing|1=Sowing}} took place in the month {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケフ・ネジェス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|January|en|January|qid=Q108|lt=January|1=January}}, from the New Kingdom, the month {{ill|Pharmouthi|ja|パルムティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharmouthi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), immediately after the coldest season, which in ancient Egypt lasted from {{ill|December|en|December|qid=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} to early January. ===Sorting=== During maturation in February and March, the flax had to be freed of {{ill|Weed|en|Weed|qid=Q101879|lt=weeds|1=Weed}} and poor plant growth, similar to {{ill|wheat|ja|小麦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wheat|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コムギ|1=Wheat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15645384}} cultivation. This activity, which lasted until harvest, was called "Sorting of the flax plants" by the Egyptians. ===Harvesting and Binding the Flax=== The harvest began at yellow ripeness, usually in the month {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス (月)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chonsu (month)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (late March, from the New Kingdom month {{ill|Pachon|ja|パコン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pachon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and lasted only eight to ten days, since at full ripeness in the month {{ill|Chenti-chet|ja|ケンティ・チェト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chenti-chet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|April|en|April|qid=Q118|lt=April|1=April}}, from the New Kingdom month {{ill|Payni|ja|パイニ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Payni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the {{ill|seed capsules|ja|種子莢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seed capsules|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} burst open. After the harvest ("Striking the flax plants"), the division into areas of use such as {{ill|Medicine|en|Medicine|qid=Q11190|lt=medicine|1=Medicine}}, clothing, and other everyday items began. In further processing, the activities of "Pulling out" and "Binding" are known, to produce the necessary {{ill|flax fiber|ja|亜麻繊維|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flax fiber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In depictions, the {{ill|Determinative|en|Determinative|qid=Q1201172|lt=determinative|1=Determinative}} of an almost upright man was chosen for aesthetic reasons to make the activity performed in a squatting posture on the harvested flax bundle appear more graceful. ===Mention of the Flax Blossom in the Old Testament=== In the {{ill|Old Testament|en|Old Testament|qid=Q19786|lt=Old Testament|1=Old Testament}}, the flax blossom (February/March) is mentioned in the context of the seventh plague among the {{ill|Ten Plagues of Egypt|ja|エジプトの十の災い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Plagues of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "The flax and the barley were ruined, because the barley was in the ear and the flax was in bloom." {{Bibel|2 Mos|9|31}} ==Literature== * {{ill|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2''. In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen, Nr. 5''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, pp. 167–170. {{translated page|de|Flachsernte (Altes Ägypten)|version=225157296|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:古代エジプトの亜麻の収穫]] [[de:Flachsernte (Altes Ägypten)]] {{wikidata link|Q1426240}} [[Category:Feste und Brauchtum (Landwirtschaft)]] [[Category:Festivals of ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest]] [[Category:Landwirtschaft (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Category:Plants in Cultural Context]] [[Category:Plants in culture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
